2605.23203.title: Lipschitz Optimization for Formal Verification of Homographies
2605.23203.paper_summary: The adoption of vision neural networks in regulated industries requires formal robustness guarantees, especially in safety-critical domains such as healthcare, autonomous vehicles, and aerospace. However, current approaches are confined to incomplete statistical verification or robustness to $\ell_p$-norm and affine transforms, which cover only a narrow subset of perturbations to the image formation process. In particular, robustness to camera motion remains an open problem despite being key to deploy many vision applications. We present a formal verification approach that targets robustness against 3D motion perturbations of the capturing camera. We first establish a closed-form mapping from camera pose to pixel values. By analyzing the continuity properties of the resulting homographies, we show that recent work on Lipschitz optimization and piecewise continuity can be extended to derive tight linear bounds on perturbed pixel values. Our approach applies to scenes with predominantly planar structure, such as ground planes in augmented reality, road markings and traffic signs in autonomous driving, or planar workspaces in robotic manipulation. This enables the first formal verification of projective geometry transforms, without complex simulation, surrogate networks, or explicit image-formation models. We validate our implementation and show up to 89% speedup and 7% tighter bounds over prior work. We then evaluate our method on the VNN-COMP benchmark and reveal systematic weaknesses to projective perturbations. Finally, we demonstrate a real-world case study on a safety-critical runway classifier, highlighting practical vulnerabilities to camera motion, and addressing a key challenge in the certification of learned models. Data and code are publicly available at https://github.com/jeangud/homography-verification .
2605.23203.paper_summary_zh: 在受監管的行業中採用視覺神經網絡需要正式的穩健性保證，尤其是在醫療保健、自動駕駛車輛和航空航天等安全關鍵領域。<lb/>然而，目前的方法僅限於不完整的統計驗證或對 $\ell_p$-範數和仿射變換的穩健性，這僅涵蓋了對圖像形成過程的狹窄擾動子集。<lb/>特別是，儘管對相機運動的穩健性對於部署許多視覺應用至關重要，但這仍然是一個未解決的問題。<lb/>我們提出了一種正式驗證方法，針對捕捉相機的三維運動擾動的穩健性。<lb/>我們首先建立了從相機姿態到像素值的封閉形式映射。<lb/>通過分析所得到的單應性質的連續性，我們展示了近期在Lipschitz優化和分段連續性方面的工作可以擴展，以推導出對擾動像素值的緊線性界限。<lb/>我們的方法適用於主要具有平面結構的場景，例如擴增實境中的地面平面、自動駕駛中的道路標記和交通標誌，或機器人操作中的平面工作空間。<lb/>這使得對投影幾何變換的首次正式驗證成為可能，無需複雜的模擬、替代網絡或明確的圖像形成模型。<lb/>我們驗證了我們的實現，並顯示出比先前工作快至89%的速度提升和7%更緊的界限。<lb/>然後，我們在VNN-COMP基準上評估我們的方法，揭示了對投影擾動的系統性弱點。<lb/>最後，我們展示了一個關於安全關鍵跑道分類器的現實案例研究，突顯了對相機運動的實際脆弱性，並解決了學習模型認證中的一個關鍵挑戰。<lb/>數據和代碼可在 https://github.com/jeangud/homography-verification 上公開獲得。
2605.23203.author: Jean-Guillaume Durand et.al.
2605.23203.authors: Jean-Guillaume Durand, Panagiotis Kouvaros, Maxime Gariel, Alessio Lomuscio
